0.1_purpose.summary: Machine-readable JSON index for the canonical responsibility-to-entity delegation matrix.
0.1_purpose.role: Groups the 49 canonical responsibilities by responsibility family, links each responsibility to its delegated entities, governed objects, enablements, surfaces, obligations, and trace coverage, and exposes entity load summaries for query and validation.
0.1_purpose.mapping_law: Responsibility / Obligation -> Primary Delegated Entity -> Supporting Entities -> Governed Objects -> Enablement Family -> Surface Type -> Trace

0.2_index_rules.responsibility_grain: one top-level responsibility per canonical delegation row
0.2_index_rules.family_grain: one family object containing the responsibility rows that belong to that family
0.2_index_rules.entity_load_grain: one entity load record per entity appearing as primary or supporting carrier
0.2_index_rules.csv_array_policy: CSV sources use semicolon-separated canonical IDs.
0.2_index_rules.json_array_policy: This JSON index uses arrays for all multi-value fields.
0.2_index_rules.actor_guard: Governed objects are not actors and must not be used as primary delegated entities.
0.2_index_rules.classification_guard: QUEUE-RUNTIME-LAYER is classification-only and must not carry primary delegation.
0.2_index_rules.metadata_policy_guard: Metadata Injector is schema/directive-governed and must not be treated as policy-consuming.

2.0_responsibility_families.summary: Ingress delegation assigns typed content admission and envelope preparation to the Envelope Assembler, with Validator and Timestamp Stabilizer participating only under their correct ingress semantics.
Policy delegation separates policy intake and assignment from mechanism hosting: PolicyAssigner assigns, while eligible components host mechanism semantics.
Metadata responsibilities are carried by Metadata Injector through schema/directive governance, not Policy Object mechanism hosting.
Operational/scaling responsibilities enter administratively, then Queue Bundle Agent realizes the directives over queue structures.
Registry/Dispatcher delegation preserves Dispatcher as actor, Registry / Delivery Port as contract surface, and Delivery-Cycle Flag Set as state artifact.
Envelope carrier responsibilities preserve the envelope as authoritative carrier while distinguishing occupancy from lifecycle authority.
Boundary/exclusion responsibilities preserve the assembler authority boundary, surface separation, template contracts, policy doctrine, dispatcher boundary, and Queue-as-Container doctrine.

Runtime state delegation assigns queue bundle orchestration, single queue moderation, slots, occupancy, lifecycle, waiting lists, and dispatcher feedback to mature runtime entities rather than a classification-only layer.

3.0_delegation_rows.anti_collapse: content ingress must not become administrative ingress; contract must not become runtime COM interface
validating and hot-path variants must not collapse
hot-path must not silently reintroduce assembler-local validation
Validator must not participate in hot-path externalized validation
Timestamp Stabilizer is mature, not placeholder; timestamp must not be optional
raw content must not remain final runtime carrier
policy bundles must not enter through consuming content ingress
Policy Object identity must not be lost or treated as executable behavior
policy assignment must be mediated; host components must not self-source policies
administrative assignment surface must not be confused with managerial execution
Policy Object governs semantics; component realizes behavior locally
policy replacement must not occur mid-unsafe operation
metadata directives must not enter through consuming ingress
Metadata Injector must not be bypassed and must not become policy host
metadata must not be treated as arbitrary content payload
metadata authority must not collapse into consuming ingress
operational directives are control input, not log content
operational directive must affect queue eligibility or progression, not content payload
write unavailability must not be hidden; waiting list stores queue-container IDs
queue order must be policy-conforming without making Policy Object executor
Dispatcher must not handoff blindly
capacity/backpressure must gate delivery when applicable
Registry / Delivery Port must not replace Dispatcher as actor
boundary-sufficient registry access must not become registry internal ownership
delivery-cycle flags are state updates, not detailed reports
Assembler must not retain envelope lifecycle authority after valid handoff
Envelope assembly must not bypass metadata or timestamp preparation
raw content must not replace Log Level Envelope as runtime carrier
envelope movement must not be confused with queue-container identity
lifecycle authority transfers only at valid dispatcher-registry handoff
occupancy binding must not imply envelope lifecycle ownership
read/query responsibilities must not become assembler responsibilities
consuming/admin/managerial surfaces must not collapse
Dispatcher remains final assembler-side boundary; port is not actor
contracts are typed/template laws, not runtime COM interfaces
Policy Object must not become executor; component must host mechanism
Queue equals Container; no separate runtime container above queue
Queue Bundle Agent must not replace Single Queue Moderator
Single Queue Moderator operates over one queue-container, not bundle level
slots must remain inside Queue Container semantics
occupancy is binding/state, not envelope lifecycle ownership
slot lifecycle is local runtime state, not registry-side envelope lifecycle
post-delivery completion must restore waiting discipline where required
writing waiting list stores queue-container identifiers, not envelopes
delivery waiting list stores queue-container identifiers, not envelopes
Queue Container Identifier locates queue-container only, not envelope
requalified queue-container ID must return to the correct waiting discipline
detailed reports must remain separate from delivery-cycle flags
ownership and occupancy language must not introduce a separate runtime container

highest_runtime_load.load_meaning: highest runtime-state load; owns one queue-container moderation, slots, occupancy, lifecycle, and requalification semantics
highest queue-bundle orchestration load; realizes operational directives and queue-container terminology at bundle level
highest boundary/delivery load; final assembler-side handoff actor and owner of flags/reports
high ingress/preparation contract load; client-side composed envelope preparation object

dispatcher_port_actor_guard.rule: Dispatcher remains actor; Registry / Delivery Port remains outbound contract surface.

6.0_query_examples.query: Which entity carries detailed dispatcher feedback reports?

6.0_query_examples.answer_join_rule: filter delegation_rows where gaps contains G-006

6.0_query_examples.query: Which responsibilities involve Log Level Envelope?
6.0_query_examples.answer_join_rule: filter delegation_rows where governed_objects contains BCO-012

6.0_query_examples.query: Which responsibilities require Queue Runtime Enablement?
6.0_query_examples.answer_join_rule: filter delegation_rows where enablements contains EN-FAM-004

6.0_query_examples.query: Which rows prove Queue Runtime Layer is not used as delegate?

8.0_final_canonical_statement.statement: This JSON index is the canonical machine-readable rollup for the delegation matrix package. It preserves responsibility coverage, family grouping, entity load, enablement mapping, governed-object boundaries, and anti-collapse validation.
non_canonical_if: a responsibility has no primary delegated entity
QUEUE-RUNTIME-LAYER appears as a primary delegate
Registry / Delivery Port replaces Dispatcher as actor
Delivery-Cycle Flag Set and Dispatcher Feedback Report are conflated
Queue Container is split from Queue
Envelope Slot Occupancy becomes lifecycle owner
